reasoning: César Cielo set the 50m freestyle world record (20.91) in 2009 during the controversial supersuit era. This record has stood for over 16 years, with the absolute best recent times still being substantially slower (e.g., Caeleb Dressel's 21.04 and Cam McEvoy's 21.06). There is extremely little chance of it being broken in Jan/Feb 2026 since elite swimmers typically peak for major summer events (Olympics / World Championships), and there are no major international long-course meets scheduled for early 2026. Furthermore, there are no news reports indicating the record was independently broken in a minor regional meet recently. Even if someone inexplicably broke it on January 19th, formal ratification by World Aquatics takes weeks to months, meaning Wikipedia might at most mark it as unratified. Hence, the probability remains at the upper bound. I project a 98% chance for late January and apply a slight time-decay to 97% for mid-February to account for the infinitesimally greater exposure to unpredictable events.
